div.head: 333. Editorial Note
div.p: On March 5 at Ankara, the United States signed bilateral Agreements of Cooperation with the Governments of Turkey, Iran, and Pakistan. These agreements were developed pursuant to the July 28, 1958, declaration signed by the member states of the Baghdad Pact at its Ministerial Meeting in London. The text of the agreement with Turkey is in 10 UST 320. For text of a Department of State press release explaining the background of these agreements, see Department of State Bulletin, March 23, 1959, pages 416–417. For text of the July 28, 1958, declaration, see Ibid., August 18, 1958, pages 272–273.
